title: aconitum lycoctonum var. altissimus
intro: Aconitum lycoctonum var. altissimus, also known as Monk's Hood, is a variety of the tall monk's hood plant, which is a perennial flowering plant native to parts of Europe, Asia, and North America. The medicinal properties of Aconitum lycoctonum var. altissimus lie in its roots, which contain aconitine, a toxic alkaloid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its potential application in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Aconitum lycoctonum var. altissimus is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion for medicinal use, but it is essential to exercise extreme caution when handling the plant due to its toxicity.
